full_text_rationale: Full text describes SARE as a research-coding agent with a persistent global training context and cumulative cheatsheet that stores reusable strategies across tasks, evaluated on SUPER, ResearchCodeBench, and ScienceAgentBench.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 013; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Balaji Dinesh Gangireddi, Aniketh Garikaparthi, Manasi Patwardhan, Arman Cohan
titulo: Self-Adapting Agents for Automating Research Coding Workflows
formato_armazenamento: Global Training Context with auxiliary context, reflection history, and cumulative cheatsheet storing recurring strategies, common fixes, domain-specific knowledge, failure modes, and reusable procedural heuristics across research-coding tasks.
framework_agente: SARE Self-Adapting Research Engineer with Actor, Reflector, Global Training Context, code-based prompt/cheatsheet editing, and cross-repository adaptation
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; offline and online adaptation; ablation; cost table
metricas_desempenho: Submission rate, landmarks, output match, overall score, ResearchCodeBench accuracy, ScienceAgentBench success rate, CodeBLEU
metricas_cl: Offline and online adaptation with training/validation/test splits, persistent Global Training Context, cumulative cheatsheet growth, reflection history, and ablations removing cheatsheet, auxiliary context, reflection, or the whole Global Training Context.
baseline_comparado: Baseline agent, Static SOTA, GEPA-style adaptation, SARE without ground truth, SARE with ground truth, and SARE ablations without cheatsheet, auxiliary context, reflection, or Global Training Context
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: The cheatsheet and Global Training Context grow during adaptation, but the paper does not report total memory size over time.
custo_por_tarefa: SUPER costs about $1-3 per task; ResearchCodeBench and ScienceAgentBench are reported at about $1 per task in the appendix cost table.
resultado_principal: With ground truth in offline adaptation, SARE reaches 88.9 submission, 38.1 landmarks, 31.4 output match, and 52.8 overall on SUPER, plus 35.41 ResearchCodeBench accuracy and 30.6 ScienceAgentBench success rate.
desempenho_vs_baseline: SARE without ground truth improves the SUPER overall score to 38.8 versus 29.9 for the baseline, while SARE with ground truth reaches 52.8. Ablating the whole Global Training Context drops SUPER overall from 34.7 to 16.0 in the reported ablation table.
custo_vs_baseline: Per-task benchmark costs are reported at roughly $1-3, but the paper does not isolate the additional cost of reflection and context growth versus baselines.
limitacoes: Reflective calls and growing Global Training Context introduce overhead and can approach context-window limits; evaluation is concentrated on research-coding benchmarks and selected baselines.
ameacas_validade: Benchmark representativeness, train/validation/test split choices, use of ground-truth traces in offline adaptation, single LLM stack, anonymous artifact status, and comparison-baseline implementation choices may affect validity.
